Details:
Ozonation was an effective process to degrade diuron from the conditions evaluated. Significant removal percentages  (73 to 100) were seen in both lab water and surface water. One study showed that doubled ozone dosage (more than required for disinfection) significantly reduced  the diuron concentration without significant bromate ion formation [1458]. 
One study showed that the effect of ozone on diuron removal can be enhanced when combined with activated carbon adsorption.
